name: Amélie DC Bistro & Wine Bar

notes: All 48 frozen rows were adjudicated: 29 exact matches, 13 normalized matches, four variant matches, and two stale rows.
The current restaurant-issued surfaces contain 99 food presentations, collapsed without loss of source traceability into 43 formulations across seven categories.

Six current formulations omitted from the frozen record were restored: Plateau Apéro, Maryland Seared Monkfish, Extra Breadbasket, the expanded side bundle, Banana Crème Brûlée, and Vanilla Crème Brûlée.

Thirty-five current formulations have positive signals supported by direct restaurant-menu ingredient terms or unambiguous formulation identity; eight remain unavailable because the restaurant does not disclose enough fixed ingredient detail.
The menus are not complete allergen matrices: absent ingredients are not negative assurances, and the server-alert instruction is not a may-contain or cross-contact statement.
The repair corrects 21 frozen allergen results: 20 underreported formulations and one overreported Grilled Octopus milk signal. All 43 current rows retain empty mayContain arrays.

Reviewed restaurant identity, all four current menu surfaces, alcohol-only exclusions, 99 extracted presentations, consolidation into 43 formulations, every frozen item mapping, direct positive allergen semantics, unavailable semantics, and final generated fields.
Eleven focused tests pass; five captured artifact hashes match; all 48 item evidence references resolve; and the generated target matches the reviewed snapshot on every core menu and allergen field.

The target-only repair is idempotent at SHA-256 afa2165cdb506ca325cbfb34ef8e343f2b2aee6fb69b14f3a852d16b0084fbe9 and preserved all 1,494 non-target restaurants at SHA-256 20adb3d1b56b18a0b5b1b18fd0d770fbdf021367604c5231e77de4fa3ace3341.

sourceAttempts.outcome: captured the current home, lunch, dinner, brunch, and happy-hour pages; parsed 99 food presentations into 43 formulations
notes: All five responses returned HTTP 200 and are hash-pinned in the evidence ledger.

sourceAttempts.outcome: no separate restaurant-issued allergen guide, matrix, or item-level cross-contact disclosure located
notes: Each menu asks guests to alert a server about allergies; that instruction is not promoted to a may-contain signal.

sourceAttempts.outcome: reconciled all 48 frozen rows against every current restaurant-issued presentation and identified 2 stale rows, 6 omitted formulations, and 21 allergen mismatches

findings.summary: The frozen 48-row record mixed surface duplicates and spelling or pricing variants, retained two stale formulations, and omitted six current formulations.
findings.resolution: Replaced the frozen rendering with 43 current formulations across 99 retained presentations; consolidated spelling, service, and pricing variants; removed both stale rows; and restored all six omitted formulations.

findings.summary: Twenty-one frozen allergen results differed from the current menu evidence: 20 underreported positive signals and one overreported signal.
findings.resolution: Corrected all 21 mismatches in the reviewed snapshot: 20 underreported positive signals and the single unsupported dairy signal on Grilled Octopus.

findings.summary: Maryland Rockfish and Local Roasted Chicken are absent from the current dinner menu, which instead publishes Maryland Seared Monkfish and Roasted Lemon Chicken.
findings.resolution: Removed Maryland Rockfish, published Maryland Seared Monkfish, and mapped the former Local Roasted Chicken row to the current Roasted Lemon Chicken formulation.

findings.summary: The restaurant instructs guests to alert a server about allergies, but that is not an item-level allergen matrix or a cross-contact disclosure.
findings.resolution: Kept the server-alert language as a source warning only; every mayContain array remains empty because no item-level cross-contact statement was published.

findings.summary: The frozen Grilled Octopus row carried milk even though the current formulation supports shellfish only; generic creamy-equals-dairy inference created an unsupported contains claim.
findings.resolution: Set Grilled Octopus to shellfish only and removed the unsupported milk contains claim.

findings.summary: The frozen record did not carry the complete current street address.
findings.resolution: Set the current display address to 1315 14th St NW, Washington, DC 20005.

repairs.summary: Replaced 48 duplicate and stale frozen rows with 43 current formulations across 99 source presentations; removed two stale formulations, restored six omitted formulations, consolidated variants, corrected 21 allergen results, preserved eight unavailable rows, and prevented the server-alert notice from becoming a cross-contact claim.

notes: Current address: 1315 14th St NW, Washington, DC 20005.
The current pages contain 99 food presentations, consolidated to 43 formulations across seven categories.
No safe dedicated audit worktree exists because the checkout remains dirty, so no checkpoint commit was created.
